title: Comtech Telecommunications Corp. Shareholders Are Encouraged to Reach Out to Johnson Fistel for More Information About Potentially Recovering Their Losses
content:
SAN DIEGO, March  19, 2026  (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Johnson Fistel, PLLP is investigating potential claims on behalf of investors of Comtech Telecommunications Corp. (NASDAQ: CMTL). The investigation focuses on Comtech executive officers and whether investor losses may be recovered under federal securities laws.

What if I purchased Comtech securities?
If you purchased Comtech securities and suffered losses on your investment, join our investigation now: Click here to join the investigation.

Or for more information, contact Jim Baker at jimb@johnsonfistel.com or (619) 814-4471.
There is no cost or obligation to you.

Background of the investigation
On March 16, 2026, Comtech announced its financial results for the second quarter of fiscal 2026. Among other things, the Company reported net sales of $106.8 million, down 15.6% year-over-year from $126.6 million in the prior-year quarter. Comtech also reported an operating loss of $1.2 million and a net loss attributable to common stockholders of $20.2 million.

Comtech further disclosed that net sales in its Satellite and Space Communications segment declined 31.3% year-over-year to $50.6 million. The Company attributed the decline primarily to its decision to phase out and eliminate certain low-margin and working-capital-intensive revenues, as well as the impact of the recent U.S. government shutdown.

In light of this disclosure, Johnson Fistel is investigating whether Comtech complied with the federal securities laws. If you suffered losses from your investment in Comtech stock, contact Johnson Fistel.

About Johnson Fistel, PLLP | Securities Fraud & Investor Rights
Johnson Fistel, PLLP is a nationally recognized shareholder-rights law firm with offices in California, New York, Georgia, Idaho, and Colorado. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in shareholder derivative and securities class action lawsuits and also assists foreign investors who purchased shares on U.S. exchanges. To learn more, visit www.johnsonfistel.com.

Achievements
In 2024, Johnson Fistel was ranked among the Top 10 Plaintiff Law Firms by ISS Securities Class Action Services. This recognition reflects the firm’s effectiveness in advocating for investors, having recovered approximately $90,725,000 for aggrieved clients in cases where it served as lead or co-lead counsel. This marks the eighth time the firm has been recognized as a top plaintiffs’ securities law firm in the United States, based on the total dollar value of final recoveries.

Attorney advertising.
Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.
Services may be performed by attorneys in any of our offices.
Johnson Fistel, PLLP has paid for the dissemination of this promotional communication, and Frank J. Johnson is the attorney responsible for its content.

title: Comtech Telecommunications Corp. Q2 2026 Earnings Call Summary
content:
Comtech Telecommunications Corp. Q2 2026 Earnings Call Summary - Moby

Strategic Transformation and Portfolio Optimization

Management successfully transitioned the business model by deliberately phasing out low-margin, working capital-intensive legacy contracts such as VSAT and GFSR. The 16% revenue decline was a calculated outcome of prioritizing 'good business' over volume, resulting in gross profit expansion from 27% to 34%. Satellite and Space (S&S) performance was temporarily hindered by the U.S. government shutdown, which delayed orders and net sales during the period. The company is migrating from non-recurring engineering (NRE) services to higher-volume production of proprietary digital common ground modems and multipath radios. Allerium growth was driven by the continued migration of Public Safety Answering Points (PSAPs) to cloud-based Next-Generation 911 platforms and recurring revenue streams. Operational efficiency initiatives and a reduced cost structure led to a 200% plus increase in adjusted EBITDA despite lower consolidated sales. Improved financial stability has created a 'positive flywheel effect,' restoring confidence among mission-critical customers, vendors, and employees.

Production Scaling and Technological Evolution

The company expects to transition into full production for a next-generation satellite modem contract during the remainder of fiscal 2026. Management anticipates production deliveries for a second next-generation product with a major prime contractor to begin within the current fiscal year. The EDIM program is positioned as a long-term successor to the EBEM modem, targeting an installed base of tens of thousands of units for potential upgrades. Allerium is approximately 75% through its transition to cloud-based infrastructure, with the new 'Mira' call handling platform expected to launch shortly. Future product development in the public safety segment will focus on AI-infused applications to synthesize complex data streams for emergency responders.

Legal Resolutions and Capital Structure

Former CEO Ken Peterman voluntarily withdrew his $41 million in claims against the company, though Comtech continues to pursue counterclaims for breach of fiduciary duty. The company successfully removed its 'going concern' disclosure following credit facility amendments and four consecutive quarters of positive operating cash flow. Liquidity remains stable at approximately $50 million, supported by improved inventory management and disciplined accounts payable practices. Financial covenants for net leverage and fixed charge coverage ratios remain suspended until the period ending January 31, 2027.

Story Continues

Analyst Question and Answer Summary

Our analysts just identified a stock with the potential to be the next Nvidia. Tell us how you invest and we'll show you why it's our #1 pick. Tap here.

Impact of fiscal discipline versus government shutdown on revenue

Management clarified that nearly all the Satellite and Space revenue decline resulted from the intentional phase-out of legacy, low-margin business. While the government shutdown caused timing delays, these were partially offset by the launch of next-generation troposcatter and digital modem products.

Market opportunity for next-generation satellite modems

The company is targeting a massive installed base for the EDIM program, which replaces a 10-year legacy platform that previously sold tens of thousands of units. One modem platform is already in low-rate production and is expected to contribute significantly to the second half of the fiscal year.

AI integration and cloud transition progress in Allerium

AI is being utilized to aggregate disparate data sources for 911 dispatchers to create a clearer emergency response picture. The segment is roughly three-quarters of the way through moving its product portfolio to private cloud and SaaS-based delivery models.
